question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of caller authentication in getHookStorage() to read the storage values of hooks belonging to any account, where those values encode transaction parameters or state, constituting unauthorized transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a null keyLowerBound or keyUpperBound in HookStorageRequest to cause a NullPointerException or unexpected query behavior in findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyBetweenAndDeletedIsFalse(), potentially leaking error details about transaction storage structure?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the keys collection in HookStorageRequest by supplying duplicate byte[] keys to cause the findByOwnerIdAndHookIdAndKeyInAndDeletedIsFalse() IN-clause to behave unexpectedly, potentially returning more results than intended and leaking transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HookStorageResult constructor receiving the full ownerId entity object to extract additional entity metadata (beyond just the storage) that reveals transaction-linked account information?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the limit parameter in getPageRequest() by setting it to 0 or a negative value to cause an exception or bypass pagination, potentially returning an unbounded result set of hook storage entries linked to transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getHookStorage() calls entityService.lookup() for both current and historical paths, and that lookup() may throw different exceptions for valid vs invalid ownerIds, to enumerate which entity IDs have active hooks and thus active transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorage()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the byte[] key comparison in getKeysInRange() — which uses Arrays.compareUnsigned() — by supplying keys of different lengths to cause incorrect range filtering, allowing keys outside the intended range to pass through and expose unintended transaction storage?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user access the full historical change log of any hook's storage by supplying a non-empty timestamp Bound, triggering getHookStorageChange() and receiving all key-value mutations linked to past transactions without any authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestamp.getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() and timestamp.adjustUpperBound() methods by supplying extreme timestamp values (e.g., 0 to Long.MAX_VALUE) to retrieve the complete historical transaction record for any hook owner?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the native SQL query in findByKeyBetweenAndTimestampBetween() — which uses 'distinct on (key)' — to receive the most recent value for each key within a timestamp range, effectively reconstructing the transaction state at any point in time without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the native SQL query in findByKeyInAndTimestampBetween() by supplying a large collection of keys to generate an unbounded IN-clause, causing database resource exhaustion and potential denial of service that prevents legitimate transaction gossip prevention?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'deleted' flag derivation in the native SQL query — '(value_written is null or length(value_written) = 0) as deleted' — to infer which transactions caused storage deletions by observing which keys appear as deleted in historical results?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the consensus_timestamp aliasing in the native SQL query (used as both 'modified_timestamp' and 'consensus_timestamp') to correlate hook storage changes with specific transaction timestamps, enabling reconstruction of transaction ordering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the keysInRange.isEmpty() && requestHasKeys early-return path in getHookStorageChange() as a negative oracle — receiving an empty result when supplied keys are out of range — to binary-search for valid key ranges and infer transaction storage structure?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Pageable parameter passed to findByKeyBetweenAndTimestampBetween() by requesting a very large page size to retrieve all historical hook storage changes in a single query, bypassing intended pagination limits and leaking full transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Sort ordering in getPageRequest() for historical queries — Sort.by(order, KEY).and(Sort.by(DESC, CONSENSUS_TIMESTAMP)) — to infer the most recent transaction timestamp for a given key by requesting DESC order and reading the first result's consensus_timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the native SQL query's lack of ORDER BY clause (relying solely on Pageable) to receive non-deterministic results that, when combined across multiple requests, reveal more transaction data than any single paginated response would show?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timestamp range parameters (timestampLowerBound, timestampUpperBound) in the native SQL query to perform a timing side-channel attack — measuring response latency for different timestamp ranges — to infer when transactions occurred for a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHookStorageChange()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 'created_timestamp' hardcoded to 0 in the native SQL query to cause downstream consumers of HookStorageResult to misinterpret the age of storage entries, potentially bypassing time-based access controls on transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hookIds.stream().filter() in-memory filtering to cause a time-of-check/time-of-use race condition where the hookIds list is modified between the filter and the repository call, leading to inconsistent results that leak transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Collection<Long> hookIds type in HooksRequest to supply Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE as hookId values, causing integer overflow or unexpected comparison behavior in the lowerBound/upperBound filter, bypassing range restrictions?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByOwnerIdAndHookIdBetween() accepts lowerBound=0 and upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE as valid defaults (from HooksRequest) to perform a full table scan of the hook table for any ownerId, leaking all transaction-linked hooks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the entityService.lookup() call to trigger an unhandled exception (e.g., EntityNotFoundException) that reveals internal system information about which entity IDs are valid, aiding in targeted transaction gossip attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Sort.by(request.getOrder(), HOOK_ID) construction to inject a malicious sort direction that causes the database to perform a full index scan in reverse, leaking the maximum hookId (most recent transaction) for a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getHooks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PageRequest.of(0, request.getLimit(), sort) always using page=0 to prevent cursor-based pagination security controls, allowing repeated enumeration of the same page with different bounds to reconstruct the full hook list for a victim account?
